items.claim: Cory Sandhagen's takedown defense percentage dropped to 56% due to being taken down 20 times by Merab Dvalishvili.
items.quote: But then all of a sudden, Morab Devali came in and took him down 20 times. So it dropped that thing way back down to 56%.

items.claim: Mario Bautista has a slight edge in the takedown game over Cory Sandhagen.
items.quote: Bautista nearly two takedowns over three rounds. Sanhagen gets one every three. So, you know, slight edge for Bautista, but not much.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis has a significant advantage in the takedown game over Paddy Pimblett.
items.quote: the takedown game goes to Saint Deni who lands like a takedown and a half per round.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett's 44% takedown defense is a liability against Benoit Saint Denis.
items.quote: So to take down defense of Patty, the batty's important here, right? 44%. That's not very good. And if he gets to the ground, he could potentially be controlled there.

items.claim: Max Holloway's higher significant strike volume gives him a statistical advantage over Conor McGregor.
items.quote: Holloway nearly seven significant strikes per minute. McGregor 5.3 per minute. So, if it goes the way that it should, if it goes five rounds as well, Holloway is going to lead by 25 to 45 significant strikes

items.claim: Max Holloway's 81% takedown defense makes it very likely the fight against Conor McGregor stays standing.
items.quote: McGregor's 66% takedown defense. Holloway 81. So, it's very likely this fight stays standing.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's lack of recent activity compared to Max Holloway favors Holloway.
items.quote: Holloway's still active. McGregor's not. That's got to favor Max Holloway.

items.claim: Alejandro Costa's 86% takedown defense will neutralize Cody Durden's wrestling.
items.quote: Costa 86% takedown defense, that'll pay off in this fight.
